economic_notes: Kebaowek Land Management Inc., Amik Nikewin Development Inc., and Kitika Nijwaswi L.P. accounted for by modified equity method (departure from PSAS). Kitika Nijwaswi L.P. owns Mikan Transport L.P. and Mitikon L.P. Migizy Odenaw Inc. fully consolidated.
notes: Audit is qualified on three bases: (1) inability to observe physical inventories of Mitikon L.P. as of March 31, 2021 affecting comparability; (2) Kebaowek Land Management Inc., Kitika Nijwaswi L.P., and Amik Nikewin Development Inc. not consolidated despite no longer meeting GBE definition (departure from PSAS); (3) PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations not adopted. Migizy Odenaw Inc. is the only fully consolidated subsidiary (year-end September 30, 2022 used). Large police-related deferred revenue ($4.4M+ for police infrastructure) and ISC unexpended funding ($22.2M) significantly inflate balance sheet. Accounts receivable jumped from $10M to $30.9M largely due to public security receivable ($5.1M) and other accounts receivable ($12M). isc_funding calculated as sum of ISC Flexible, Fixed, Set, and Block contributions gross before unexpended adjustments; total_government_transfers includes ISC contributions, CMHC, Federal other, FNEC, FNQLHSSC, Government of Quebec, FNHRDCQ, Secretariat, and Provincial/federal police net of deferred adjustments — note these are presented net of deferrals in the statement.
